steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the Defoe passage. Identify what the passage actually does: it compares a sermon (spoken, temporary, memory-limited) to a printed book (permanent record, always retrievable, survives the author).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It recounts why the works of some artists are still discussed by critics after the artists' deaths while others are not.: Critics are never mentioned. ✗
  • Choice B — It analyzes the contributions to society of two different groups of scholars.: No scholars or social contributions appear. ✗
  • Choice C — It demonstrates that one form of communication is more commonly used than another.: Usage frequency is never discussed. ✗
  • Choice D — It describes differences in the characteristics of two kinds of communication.: Exactly right — the passage contrasts the ephemeral nature of sermons with the permanence of printed books. ✓
That is the correct answer.
